steps.instruction: Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water
steps.tip: Always start with clean hands when handling food

steps.instruction: Lay out 2 slices of bread on a clean cutting board
Spread condiments on one or both slices
steps.tip: Not too much - you don't want it dripping!

steps.instruction: Layer meat, cheese, lettuce, and tomato
steps.tip: Put wet ingredients in the middle to prevent soggy bread

steps.instruction: Place second slice on top and cut in half diagonally
Clean up your workspace
steps.tip: Put away ingredients and wipe down surfaces

steps.instruction: Wash hands and gather all ingredients
Crack eggs into bowl and add milk
steps.tip: Crack eggs on flat surface, not bowl edge

steps.instruction: Whisk eggs and milk until no streaks remain
steps.tip: Well-mixed = fluffy eggs!

steps.instruction: Heat pan on medium heat and add butter
steps.safety: Never leave stove unattended. Medium heat prevents burning

steps.instruction: When butter melts and bubbles, pour in egg mixture
Gently stir with spatula, pushing eggs from edges to center
steps.tip: Slow stirring makes creamier eggs

steps.instruction: Remove from heat when eggs are just set but still moist
steps.tip: They'll continue cooking from residual heat

steps.instruction: Season with salt and pepper
steps.tip: Season at the end for best flavor

steps.instruction: Turn off stove and serve immediately
Read entire recipe before starting
steps.tip: Understanding all steps prevents mistakes

steps.instruction: Fill large pot 3/4 full with water and place on high heat
Cover pot and wait for water to boil
steps.tip: Covering makes it boil faster

steps.instruction: When boiling, add salt and pasta
steps.safety: Steam is very hot! Stand back when adding pasta

steps.instruction: Stir pasta to prevent sticking, then cook 8-10 minutes
steps.tip: Check package for exact cooking time

steps.instruction: While pasta cooks, heat sauce in separate pot on low heat
steps.tip: Stir occasionally to prevent scorching

steps.instruction: Test pasta doneness by tasting one piece
Place colander in sink and carefully pour pasta through
steps.safety: Pour AWAY from you! Hot steam can burn

steps.instruction: Return pasta to pot and add heated sauce
Mix well and serve with parmesan if desired
Turn off both burners and clean up
Wash hands thoroughly
Rinse all vegetables under cold running water
steps.tip: Even pre-washed greens should be rinsed

steps.instruction: Tear lettuce into bite-sized pieces and place in bowl
steps.tip: Tearing is better than cutting - stays fresher

steps.instruction: Slice cucumber and tomatoes on cutting board
steps.safety: Keep fingers curled away from blade

steps.instruction: Peel and shred carrot
steps.tip: Use vegetable peeler for thin, even strips

steps.instruction: Add all vegetables to bowl and toss gently
Add dressing just before serving
steps.tip: Too early makes salad soggy

steps.instruction: Wash cutting board and knife immediately
steps.tip: Prevents bacteria growth

steps.instruction: Read soup label for specific instructions
Open can with can opener carefully
Pour soup concentrate into pot
Add water or milk as directed on can
steps.tip: Use the empty can to measure

steps.instruction: Stir well to combine
Heat on medium, stirring frequently
When soup starts to simmer, reduce to low heat
steps.tip: Simmer = tiny bubbles, not rolling boil

steps.instruction: Heat for 3-5 more minutes, stirring occasionally
Test temperature carefully before serving
steps.safety: Soup retains heat - let cool slightly

steps.instruction: Turn off stove, ladle into bowl, serve with crackers
Butter one side of each bread slice
Place cheese between unbuttered sides
Heat pan on medium-low
Place sandwich in pan, buttered side down
Cook 3-4 minutes until golden brown
Flip carefully and cook other side
steps.tip: Press gently with spatula for even browning

steps.instruction: Remove when both sides are golden and cheese is melted
Turn off stove and let cool 1 minute before eating
